overview.value: Echeveria is a compact rosette succulent with powdery leaves. It needs strong light and dry soil to stay colorful and symmetrical.

careDifficultyDescription.value: Simple in strong light, but stretches quickly in dim rooms.

light.value: Very bright light with gentle direct sun.

water.value: Water only when soil is completely dry, usually every 2-3 weeks.

humidityDescription.value: Prefers dry air and good airflow.

diseaseRiskDescription.value: Overwatering and water trapped in the rosette can cause rot.

fertilizer.value: Feed once or twice during spring and summer with diluted succulent fertilizer.

tips.value: If the rosette stretches upward, it needs more light.
